mission.title: backstage: [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework
mission.description: [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework. Requested by 33+ users.

steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
**Status:** Open for comments

## Need

TechDocs is a centralized platform for publishing, viewing, and discovering technical documentation across an entire organization.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework
steps.description:
### Configuration matrix

Depending on each addon location and addon scope, there is a need to configure each combination. The table below categorizes the intended options given to both app integrators and documentation creators. 

|                                                           | `GLOBAL` scope     | `SITE` scope     | `PAGE` scope     |
| ----------------------------------- | -------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------- |
| Virtual location `COMPONENT`   |  <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | <sub>(not applicable)<sup>5</sup></sub>  | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + Use addon tag in Markdown content<sup>4</sup>  |
| Virtual location `CONTENT`         | Register in routes<sup>1</sup>     | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + register in `mkdocs.yml`<sup>3</sup>  | Register in scope<sup>2</sup> + register in `mkdocs.yml`<sup>3</sup> + Use addon tag in Markdown content<sup>4</sup>  |

The RFC was implemented in PR #10203 ([TechDocs] Initial Addon Framework Implementation), merged March 2022.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] TechDocs Addons Framework" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The TechDocs Addons Framework RFC (issue #9636) was a proposal to extend TechDocs with a pluggable addon system. The RFC was accepted and implemented in PR #10203 ([TechDocs] Initial Addon Framework Implementation), merged March 2022. The framework allows integrators to register addons at various scopes (GLOBAL, SITE, PAGE) and virtual locations (COMPONENT, CONTENT).
